reviews.review_msg: Took this in Fall this year. Suzanne is a very knowledgeable professor, however, her course is quite heavy with lots of homework. Just make sure you have enough time because you need to submit multiple assignments every week!

Dr. Hilgendorf is a good professor. She's patient and respectful.The material for a single course is a bit too much and you can't pass exams just by skimming through the textbook. You must work hard to understand it. Lucky for me I took it last term when Zenita was the TA and she helped me a lot. But avoid the course in busy terms if you can.

Hated the short podcast question assignments, says she listens to feedback but doesn't budge despite various complaints about the format. I just skip through the podcast until I find the assignment questions, big waste of time. She is knowledgeable but does not give a lot of actual help to students. Only take the course if you have time for this.

Racist but if that doesn't matter to you. she missed much of our class time going to conferences or sick but wouldn't cut students who miss class for similar reasons any slack.Asks for anonymous feedback but wouldn't take any into consideration. lots of work,( didn't mind it) but if you think you'll get sick or go through any hardship dont take it.

I was really excited to take this class because of the subject matter, but I've been quite disappointed in the way that she structures her material.
There are no clear instructions or rubric for any of the assignments she gives (5 every week) other than to listen to a podcast that may or may not give some direction.

Professor Hilgendorf was frustrating to learn from. She gives at least five small assignments each week, plus online chapter discussions. She gives zero rubrics, solid criteria or word counts for these assignments. You never know what she wants. Way too intense for a first-year course. Interesting subject but not worth learning about from her.

WARNING: The last test had an undiclosed segment that was different from the material discussed in class and textbook; 
Doesn't seem to care much. The pre-recorded lectures were of poor audio quality. The in-person lectures were long-winded; Likes to go on tangents whenever students ask simple questions, as if not sure of the answer herself.

The assignments were graded without any criteria and without much explanation in class or on canvas. If a university professor or an authoritative scholar in linguistics fails to meet the care and compassion of a student, it is better to retake the education course. A bit of the basic qualities and standards of teachers are not there.

Way too many assignments a week (4-7), hours of podcasts per week and attendance was mandatory. I found it frustrating if a student were to miss a class it would be a problem, but office hours were cancelled more than once.

The course content was very interesting; however, there was too much homework to be able to enjoy the topics. There are discussion posts for all 13 chapters, about 6 participation assignments per week, 4 tests, and a 12 page paper. The grading was very harsh and so much work. I would avoid this class if you can or take it during a lighter semester.

Subject matter very interesting, and instructor is very knowledgeable on the topic. Crazy workload for one course, though. Each week there are multiple, not difficult, but quite time-consuming assignments to complete. She also has her own grading scale (60% is a pass not 50%) which is.. extremely irritating considering my 79% is now a C+ not a B.

Very interesting material but so much work even for a W course. She sets up two 30 min lectures a week which is a waste of time frankly speaking. Most of the time she is just explaining how exam procedures are going to be conducted or what we are suppose to do for assignments and not the material itself.

Class consisted of 3 midterms, online discussion and in-class assignments. Midterms were mc and were way too long tbh since each question you were literally given a paragraph to read and the time given wasn't enough. Online discussions were weirdly graded as it seemed like it was graded on "effort" and to get 90+, I had to spend at least 2-3 hours

LING160 had 2 canvas discussions due per week, 48 hours apart, which needed at least 2 hrs in order to get at least a 90% grade per discussion, not to mention the extra hour to read the chapter first, totalling 6 hours just in homework, not to mention the 3 hours of lecture which involve in-class assignments. Never worked this hard for a 100 level

she didnt finish the material due to her personal reason, so she gives us two writing assignment. I mean she gives us extra exercise and homework all the time during the class. However, she never tell you how she grade the exercises. She always talk about her germany story and make some question on the exam. WHO CARE?

Hilgendorf gives out extra homework all the time. She assigned us an extra 2 page paper between when our first and second drafts of our final paper were due. She bases her exams heavily on the textbook and doesn't provide any notes. Her grading criteria is not clear and she doesn't often explain what is expected of each assignment.

She passionate but her teaching style is just too traditional to be interesting. Read the textbooks for main ideas, her lectures only provide additional information, examples mentioned in lectures would usually appear on the exam. Multiple choice exams, super long and tricky. Attendance is graded.

Meh, I thought I would have liked this course more. Dr. HIlgendorf was okay, the beginning was slow and dry. Unless you can pull out a 101 examples about your multilingualism and how you speak Punjabi at home, Mandarin at work, and English and Spanish at school and don't mind speaking in front of your 80 person lecture, " u gunna have a bad time".

Overall she was decent.  Good lecturer, but slow.  Three MC exams (25% each, mostly textbook based), a graded online discussion per chapter, 5% attendance (she takes roll so don't skip).  One thing to note was that she assigned two group projects that weren't on the syllabus, with about a week to complete each.  Also, her grading scale is high.

Very clear and engaging lectures. She is understanding and available for help. Attendance is taken. Tests are multiple choice and based on the textbook. Online discussion participation based on textbook  is required and graded. Material was very interesting. Loved it and would recommend!

Approachable prof who is very passionate about her subject. Really makes sure her students understand the concepts. Only flaw is she treats her class as if it is the only one students are taking and has a tendency to add additional homework assignments at the last minute. Attendance is worth 10% so don't take this class with her if you tend to skip

I took Ling 410 with her and I felt kind of intimidated because it is a 400 level course but Suzanne is so approachable that it turned out to be my favorite linguistics class.The class is discussion based where everyone is encouraged to participate whether in class or online. Her marking scheme is very fair and she gives a lot of feedback. GREAT!

Professor Hilgendorf was amazing. Admittedly, this course (Sociolinguistics)  is her specialty, so I expected nothing short of spectacular which she delivered. This class was writing intensive and her expectations were very clear and she took alot of time to help us meet that expectation. Amazing lecturer. Willing and available for help.
a GPA booster! Highly recommend to take it!

she is born and raised in USA but have a german background and studied and stayed in germany for a few years.She may not be the best prof for german.She is very slow in updating the grades and her classes are not that interesting.But her marking seems fair and not that hard to ace the course if you go to every single class.

I liked her lectures and the pace that she went through her lectures was alright.  It could be boring at times though so I just played games on my phone or did homework for other class.  An easy marker who will give you Bs or As easily.
Her classes are so easy.  She's so easy going and pretty flexible with anything.

Not a great teacher but ok.  I did not continue on to the next level since she was teaching it.  I'm sure she's a good professor but her specialty is in linguistics and not really in German.  Her background is german but  she grew up in California for most of her life time.
hot

She is very insightful and her lectures are very interesting. The exams and assignments are quite difficult but any 400-level courses demand a lot of work. She is picky with attendance so make sure you make it to class. Also, her grading scale is quite high. 93 and up is an A.

i think she is good at teaching because she teaches at the speed of a snail. it takes her 1-2 weeks to teach one chapter. that is good for students because there is less to study. HOWEVER, she seems very reluctant to take her time outside of class or office hours to help students. plus, she tends to introduce new "parsing labels" ON EXAM DAYS. ugh
Anyway, she is hot. but I did all work back my home and never showed up into class, her class is not hard at all
She is absolutely useless and boring. She has no interest in what she's teaching. She shows up late to every class. She is not prepared. Lectures notes are not available. Avoid!
No Comments
